title: Set Up and Manage Azure Service Principal Access

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
Some Atlas features such as [federated database instances](atlas-data-federation) require access to resources in your Azure (Microsoft Azure)
Blob Storage environment. To grant access in a secure manner, create or
update an Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Service Principal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/azure/create-azure-service-principal-azureps)
with access policy.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To configure Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal access, you must have
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
- An Atlas account.

- The [Azure PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/azure/) or the [Azure
  CLI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/).

sections.heading: Set Up Azure Service Principal Access
sections.body:
You can set up an Azure (Microsoft Azure) service principal with access policy for your
Atlas project from the Atlas Administration API or Atlas UI.

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

You can use the Azure (Microsoft Azure) PowerShell or CLI (command-line
interface) to set up the Service Principal.

sections.heading: Step 1: Configure  Service Principal access.
sections.body:
Click **Configure** for Azure (Microsoft Azure).

Atlas displays the **Authorize Azure Service
Principal** page the first time. Subsequently, Atlas
displays the **Azure Service Principal Access**
page.

sections.heading: Step 2: Start the configuration for  to access your  account.
sections.body:
- Click **Authorize Azure Service Principal**.

- Review the information in the **Overview** and
  click **Next**.

sections.heading: Step 3: Assign  a relationship to your  account.
sections.body:
The **Add Atlas to your Azure Service Principal**
section displays the *AppId* that you can use with your
command to create your new or add to existing Service
Principal for Atlas. You can use either Azure (Microsoft Azure)
PowerShell or CLI to create or update the Service Principal.
To create a new Service Principal, you can follow the step
in the **Authorize Azure Service Principal** window.

**Powershell:**

PowerShell

- In a terminal, log into PowerShell and get the
  tenant ID using the commands displayed in the UI.

- Enter the ID of your Azure (Microsoft Azure) Active Directory
  tenant in the UI text field.

- Copy and run the PowerShell command displayed in
  the UI in your PowerShell to create a new Service
  Principal for Atlas.

  Atlas automatically uses the *AppId* displayed
  in the **Add Atlas to your Azure Service
  Principal** section as the value for the
  `-AppId` option.

- Enter the ID of the service principal that you
  created.

  The service principal ID is the value of the
  `ObjectId` field in your PowerShell command
  output.

**Azurecli:**

Azure CLI

- Get the ID of your Azure (Microsoft Azure) Active Directory tenant
  using the displayed command and enter the tenant ID
  returned by the command in the text field in the UI.

- Copy and run the displayed command in the Azure (Microsoft Azure)
  CLI to create a new Service Principal for
  Atlas.

  Atlas automatically uses the *AppId* displayed
  in the **Add Atlas to your Azure Service
  Principal** section as the value for `--id`
  option.

- Enter the ID of the service principal that you
  created.

  The service principal ID is the value of
  the `id` field in the command output.

sections.heading: Step 4: Click  to allow  to establish the relationship.
sections.body:
**Api:**

API

To create an Azure (Microsoft Azure) service principal with access policy for your
Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send `POST` requests to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access)
endpoint first with the ID of the project for which you wish to
create a service principal and then with the ID of the project and
service principal for which you set up access. To learn more about
the request path and body parameters, see [Create
One Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupcloudprovideraccess)
and [Authorize One Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-authorizecloudprovideraccessrole).

sections.heading: View Authorized Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principals
sections.body:
You can view all Azure (Microsoft Azure) service principals for your Atlas project
from the Atlas Administration API or Atlas UI.

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: View the authorized  service principals.
sections.body:
The **Azure Service Principal Access** page
displays. The page displays the list of services principals
configured for your project. For each service principal, the
page displays the following:

| Field Name        | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Service Principal | The ID of the service principal.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Created Date      | The date when you created and associated the service principal with Atlas.                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Actions           | The actions that you can take on the associated service principal. You can do the following:Click to remove the service principal authorization for Atlas.Click to view service principal details or create a federated database instance. |

**Api:**

API

To view all the Azure (Microsoft Azure) service principals with access policy for
your Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `GET` request to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access)
endpoint with the ID of the project. To learn more about the
syntax and parameters to include in the request, see
[Return All Cloud Provider Access Roles](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listcloudprovideraccessroles).

sections.heading: View Authorized Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal Details
sections.body:
You can view the details of an authorized Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal from
the Atlas Administration API or Atlas UI.

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: View the authorized  service principals.
sections.body:
The **Azure Service Principal Access** page
displays. The page displays the list of services principals
configured for your project.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click the ellipsis () for the Service Principal and select  from the dropdown.
sections.body:
Atlas displays the **Service Principal Details**
window. You can view and copy the following information
about the authorized Service Principal:

| Field Name           | Description                                                       |
| -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Atlas Azure AppID    | The ID of the Atlas Azure (Microsoft Azure) application.          |
| Tenant ID            | The ID of of your Azure (Microsoft Azure) Active Directory tenant |
| Service Principal ID | The ID of the service principal.                                  |

sections.heading: Step 3: Click  to close the window.
sections.body:
**Api:**

API

To view an Azure (Microsoft Azure) service principal with access policy for your
Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `GET` request to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access)
endpoint with the ID of the project. To learn more about the
syntax and parameters to include in the request, see
[Return specified Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getcloudprovideraccessrole).

sections.heading: Remove Authorized Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principal
sections.body:
You can't remove a Service Principal that is currently in use. You can
remove unused Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Principals from the Atlas Administration API or
Atlas UI.

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: View the authorized  service principals.
sections.body:
The **Azure Service Principal Access** page
displays. The page displays the list of services principals
configured for your project.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click the  for the Service Principal that you wish to remove.
Step 3: Enter the ID of the Service Principal that is shown in the window and click  to remove it.
sections.body:
**Api:**

API

To remove an Azure (Microsoft Azure) service principal with access policy for your
Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `DELETE` request to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access)
endpoint with the ID of the project, the name of the cloud
provider, and the ID of the service principal that you wish to
remove. To learn more about the syntax and parameters to include
in the request, see
[Deauthorize One Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deauthorizecloudprovideraccessrole).
